 {"id":"bd-6e9.9","title":"Bubbles Widget Test Suite","description":"Comprehensive test suite for all bubbles widgets. This ensures every widget is thoroughly tested with unit tests, property tests, integration tests, and e2e scenarios.

## Testing Philosophy
Every widget must be tested at multiple levels:
1. **Unit tests** - Individual method correctness
2. **Property tests** - Invariant verification with random inputs
3. **Integration tests** - Widget composition and interaction
4. **E2E tests** - Full user workflow simulation
5. **Snapshot tests** - Visual regression detection

## Test Requirements by Widget

### TextArea (bd-6e9.1)
Unit tests:
- `test_insert_single_char` - Insert at various cursor positions
- `test_insert_multiline` - Insert text containing newlines
- `test_delete_backward` - Backspace at line start joins lines
- `test_delete_forward` - Delete at line end joins lines
- `test_cursor_movement_horizontal` - Left/right wraps at line boundaries
- `test_cursor_movement_vertical` - Up/down maintains visual column
- `test_selection_shift_arrow` - Selection with shift+arrows
- `test_selection_word` - Double-click word selection
- `test_selection_line` - Triple-click line selection
- `test_undo_single_char` - Undo groups by word boundaries
- `test_undo_paste` - Paste is single undo unit
- `test_redo_after_edit` - Redo branch is discarded on new edit
- `test_word_wrap_soft` - Soft wrap doesn't modify buffer
- `test_word_wrap_hard` - Hard wrap inserts actual newlines
- `test_viewport_scroll_cursor_follow` - Viewport follows cursor
- `test_unicode_grapheme_cluster` - ZWJ sequences treated as single char
- `test_char_limit_enforcement` - Can't exceed limit
- `test_validation_callback` - Validation prevents invalid input

Property tests:
- `prop_insert_delete_inverse` - Insert then delete restores state
- `prop_undo_redo_inverse` - Undo then redo restores state
- `prop_cursor_always_valid` - Cursor never outside buffer bounds
- `prop_selection_ordered` - Selection start <= end

Integration tests:
- `test_textarea_in_form` - TextArea with other form widgets
- `test_textarea_focus_blur` - Focus/blur event handling
- `test_textarea_bracketed_paste` - Large paste via bracketed paste

E2E tests:
- `e2e_edit_document` - Open, edit, save workflow
- `e2e_search_replace` - Find and replace across document

### Help Widget (bd-6e9.3)
Unit tests:
- `test_single_binding_render` - Key + description formatting
- `test_multiple_bindings_horizontal` - Separator between bindings
- `test_binding_truncation` - Ellipsis when exceeds width
- `test_grouped_bindings` - Group headers and sections
- `test_full_help_toggle` - Short vs full help modes

### Paginator (bd-6e9.4)
Unit tests:
- `test_dot_pagination_render` - Correct dots for each page
- `test_numeric_pagination` - 'N/M' format
- `test_range_pagination_ellipsis` - '1 2 3 ... 10' format
- `test_page_navigation_bounds` - Can't go below 0 or above max
- `test_items_on_page_calculation` - Correct item ranges

Property tests:
- `prop_page_always_valid` - Page number always in valid range

### Cursor Utilities (bd-6e9.7)
Unit tests:
- `test_visual_column_tabs` - Tab width affects visual column
- `test_visual_column_wide_chars` - CJK chars are 2 columns
- `test_move_word_left` - Stops at word boundaries
- `test_move_word_right` - Skips punctuation correctly
- `test_byte_to_char_offset` - Correct conversion for UTF-8

Property tests:
- `prop_byte_char_roundtrip` - byte->char->byte is identity
- `prop_visual_always_positive` - Visual column >= 0

### runeutil (bd-6e9.8)
Unit tests:
- `test_grapheme_count_ascii` - ASCII string length
- `test_grapheme_count_emoji` - ZWJ family is 1 grapheme
- `test_grapheme_slice` - Correct slicing at grapheme boundaries
- `test_word_boundary_english` - Word splits on spaces/punctuation
- `test_word_boundary_cjk` - CJK characters are word boundaries
- `test_display_width_ascii` - ASCII is 1 cell per char
- `test_display_width_cjk` - CJK is 2 cells per char
- `test_truncate_to_width` - Truncates at grapheme boundaries

Property tests:
- `prop_truncate_never_exceeds` - Result width <= max_width
- `prop_grapheme_count_non_negative` - Count >= 0

## Logging Requirements
All tests must use structured logging with:
- `tracing::debug!` for test setup/teardown
- `tracing::info!` for key assertions
- `tracing::warn!` for unexpected but non-fatal conditions
- Test failures dump full state snapshot to logs

## Coverage Targets
- Line coverage: >= 90%
- Branch coverage: >= 85%
- Mutation testing survival: <= 20%

## Test Harness
Use `ftui-test` harness that provides:
- Deterministic time (frozen or controlled advancement)
- Mock terminal capabilities
- Input simulation API
- Output capture and assertion

Dependencies: All bubbles widgets, Testing infrastructure (bd-10i.11)


## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Snapshot tests cover each bubbles widget’s core render behavior.
- [ ] Interaction/unit tests cover state machines (timer/stopwatch/text editing).
- [ ] Tests are deterministic and failure output is actionable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:15:20.100943766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:58:52.510239959Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:58:52.510213489Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-6e9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-6e9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-6e9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-6e9.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-6e9.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":495,"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"},{"id":496,"issue_id":"bd-6e9.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Widget test suite significantly expanded: textarea 46 tests (was 17, includes 4 proptests), block 25 (was 7), padding 18 (was 6), paginator 25 (was 5). Total ftui-widgets: 663 unit tests passing. Property tests cover cursor validity, undo/redo invariants, selection ordering, and insert/delete inverse.","created_at":"2026-02-02T01:58:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6qxh","title":"Frame Caching with Smart Invalidation","description":"# Frame Caching with Smart Invalidation

## Background & Motivation

Many widgets are static most of the time:
- Headers and footers
- Borders and decorations
- Rarely-changing status bars

Re-rendering them every frame wastes CPU.

**The solution:**
- Cache rendered widget output
- Invalidate on model change or resize
- Composite cached content with live content

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-10i.3.6 (Buffer with scissor/opacity stacks)
- Complements bd-1iqn (Render Budget)

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct CachedWidget<W: Widget> {
    inner: W,
    cache: Option<CachedBuffer>,
    last_area: Option<Rect>,
    dirty: bool,
}

pub struct CachedBuffer {
    buffer: Buffer,
    hash: u64,  // Content hash for change detection
}

impl<W: Widget> CachedWidget<W> {
    pub fn new(widget: W) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn mark_dirty(&mut self) { self.dirty = true; }
    
    pub fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let needs_render = self.dirty 
            || self.last_area != Some(area)
            || self.cache.is_none();
            
        if needs_render {
            let mut cache_buf = Buffer::empty(area);
            self.inner.render(area, &mut cache_buf);
            self.cache = Some(CachedBuffer::new(cache_buf));
            self.last_area = Some(area);
            self.dirty = false;
        }
        
        if let Some(cache) = &self.cache {
            buf.merge(&cache.buffer);
        }
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Cache hit (TRACE level)
tracing::trace!(widget = name, "Cache hit, using cached buffer");

// Cache miss (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(widget = name, reason = ?reason, "Cache miss, re-rendering");

// Cache invalidated (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(widget = name, "Cache invalidated via mark_dirty()");
```

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Test cache hit rate
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-stats --duration 10s --static-widgets 5
# Assert: cache hit rate > 95%

# Test memory bounds
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-memory --cached-widgets 100
# Assert: memory < 10MB for 100 widgets

# Verify correctness
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-verify --compare-to-uncached
# Assert: output identical
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CachedWidget<W> wrapper implemented
- [ ] Caching works for static widgets
- [ ] Invalidation on mark_dirty()
- [ ] Resize triggers re-render
- [ ] Memory usage bounded
- [ ] Content hash detects changes
- [ ] Integration with widget tree
- [ ] No stale content ever displayed
- [ ] All tests pass","notes":"## Cache Safety Guidelines

### When to Use CachedWidget
- Static content (headers, footers, borders)
- Content that changes infrequently
- Expensive rendering operations

### When NOT to Use CachedWidget
- Dynamic content (logs, timers, animations)
- Content that depends on external state
- Small/fast widgets (caching overhead > render cost)

### Cache Coherence
The cache can become stale if:
1. Widget props change without mark_dirty()
2. External state affects rendering
3. Theme/style changes globally

Solution: Use automatic dirty detection via content hash:
```rust
impl<W: Widget + Hash> CachedWidget<W> {
    fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let current_hash = self.inner.content_hash();
        
        let needs_render = self.dirty
            || self.last_area != Some(area)
            || self.cache.as_ref().map(|c| c.hash) != Some(current_hash);
        
        // ...
    }
}
```

### Memory Management
```rust
impl<W: Widget> CachedWidget<W> {
    /// Drop cache to free memory
    pub fn clear_cache(&mut self) {
        self.cache = None;
    }
    
    /// Check cache memory usage
    pub fn cache_size(&self) -> usize {
        self.cache.as_ref().map(|c| c.buffer.memory_size()).unwrap_or(0)
    }
}
```

This should be added to the implementation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:12:19.556101145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:08:35.783580020Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:08:35.783464452Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6qxh","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-6sqaq","title":"Diagram Search & Filter (highlight/dim nodes)","description":"## Goal
Add search functionality within rendered diagrams. Type / to open search bar, filter nodes by label text. Matching nodes highlight, non-matching nodes dim to 50% opacity. Press Enter to cycle through matches.

## Why
- Makes large diagrams navigable — find the node you care about without scanning the entire diagram.
- Builds on the Buffer's opacity stack (scissor/opacity already supported).
- Simple, well-understood UX pattern (Ctrl+F in any editor).
- Combines well with interactive navigation (idea #3): search finds nodes, navigation explores from there.

## Features
1. / opens search input bar (bottom of diagram area)
2. As user types, nodes with matching labels highlight (accent border + full opacity)
3. Non-matching nodes dim to 50% opacity
4. Non-matching edges dim to 30% opacity
5. Enter cycles to next match, Shift+Enter to previous
6. Esc clears search and restores full opacity
7. Match count displayed: "3/12 nodes match"
8. Can filter by: label text (default), shape type (:diamond), cluster name (@cluster)

## Implementation
- In MermaidShowcaseState: search_active, search_query, search_matches
- During rendering: apply opacity multiplier to non-matching nodes/edges
- Use existing Buffer opacity stack for dimming

## Dependencies
- Interactive node navigation (bd-w5vn5) for shared selection state

## Files
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/mermaid_showcase.rs (search UI + state)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (opacity-based dimming for non-matches)

## Tests
- Unit: search "foo" matches nodes containing "foo"
- Unit: shape filter ":diamond" matches only diamond nodes
- Snapshot: diagram with search active showing highlighted matches","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"CobaltHollow","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:29:49.300320216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:46:01.199238132Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:46:01.199218325Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["interactivity","mermaid","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6sqaq","depends_on_id":"bd-w5vn5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:16.555460062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":807,"issue_id":"bd-6sqaq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltHollow: Committed diagram search & filter (114845d3). Search input captures chars, matches nodes by id/label, highlights current match via SelectionState, dims non-matching node cells to ~33% brightness using viewport-aware bounding box math. Search bar at bottom shows query + [n/m] count. Enter/n/N cycle matches, Backspace edits, Esc exits. 157 mermaid showcase tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:45:56Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-70rmj","title":"Smart Edge Bundling for Dense Graphs","description":"## Goal
Bundle parallel edges between the same node groups to reduce visual clutter on dense graphs. Instead of 5 separate edges between two subgraphs, show a single thick bundled edge with "×5" label.

## Motivation
Microservice architectures and complex dependency graphs often have many edges between the same clusters. Individual edge rendering creates a "hairball" that's unreadable. Edge bundling is the standard solution in graph visualization.

## Algorithm
1. Post-layout, group edges by (source_cluster, target_cluster) or (source_node, target_node).
2. For groups with 3+ parallel edges: replace with a single bundled edge.
3. Bundle edge: thicker line (or double line), label showing "×N", color blended from constituent edges.
4. For 2 parallel edges: keep separate but offset slightly to avoid overlap.
5. Self-referencing bundles (within same cluster): render as a curved loop.

## Integration Points
- Runs as a post-processing step after layout, before rendering.
- Operates on RoutedEdge list from mermaid_layout.rs.
- Bundling is optional (config flag) — some users want to see all individual edges.

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_layout.rs (add bundle_edges post-processing)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (render bundled edges with thickness + label)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid.rs (add bundling config to MermaidConfig)

## Tests
- Unit: 5 parallel A→B edges become 1 bundle with "×5"
- Unit: 2 parallel edges kept separate but offset
- Snapshot: dense graph before/after bundling","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:28:44.020939115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:18:55.096621647Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","mermaid","rendering"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-70rmj","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:17.886630978Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":801,"issue_id":"bd-70rmj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCreek claiming. Will implement smart edge bundling: post-layout grouping of parallel edges, bundle rendering with thickness + ×N labels, and config flag.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:51:11Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-7nm3","title":"Virtualized List: Height Prediction (Bayes + Conformal Bounds)","description":"## Goal
Predict unseen row heights to avoid scroll jumps and reflow hitches, with formal uncertainty bounds.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Use a Bayesian online model for row height given metadata (item type, text length, wrapping). Maintain posterior for mean/variance and use conformal prediction to bound actual height:
- Posterior: h_i ~ Normal(mu_i, sigma_i^2) with conjugate updates.
- Conformal interval: [q_alpha_lower, q_alpha_upper] from calibration residuals.

Use bounds to pre-allocate scroll space; adjust only when actual height exits interval.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scroll position remains stable; rare adjustments when bounds violated.
- Formal coverage: empirical violation rate <= alpha + tolerance.
- Deterministic under fixed seed and recorded data.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_posterior_update: mu/sigma update with new measurement.\n- unit_conformal_bounds: interval from calibration set matches alpha.\n- property_coverage: empirical violation rate <= alpha + tol.\n- e2e_scroll_stability: scripted scroll with delayed height measurements; log corrections.

## Perf
- Microbench height prediction per item; must be < 2us p95.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:13:23.383079062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:04:26.029520674Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:04:26.029489835Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7u8","title":"Implement cursor save/restore strategy (DEC vs ANSI vs emulated)","description":"# Implement Cursor Save/Restore Strategy

## Background
Plan Section 9.3 describes a critical cursor save/restore strategy for inline mode robustness. Terminals differ in which save/restore sequences they support reliably.

## Problem Statement
Inline mode requires saving cursor position before drawing UI and restoring after. However:
- DEC save/restore (\`ESC 7\` / \`ESC 8\`) is preferred but not universal
- ANSI save/restore (\`CSI s\` / \`CSI u\`) is an alternative
- Some terminals/muxes have quirks with either
- Must handle failures gracefully

## Layered Strategy (from Plan)

### Layer 1: DEC Save/Restore (Preferred)
\`\`\`rust
const DEC_SAVE: &[u8] = b"\x1b7";    // ESC 7 (DECSC)
const DEC_RESTORE: &[u8] = b"\x1b8"; // ESC 8 (DECRC)
\`\`\`
- Most widely supported on modern terminals
- Saves more state (cursor position, attributes, charset)
- Works in tmux/screen with passthrough

### Layer 2: ANSI Save/Restore (Fallback)
\`\`\`rust
const ANSI_SAVE: &[u8] = b"\x1b[s";    // CSI s
const ANSI_RESTORE: &[u8] = b"\x1b[u"; // CSI u
\`\`\`
- Less widely supported
- Only saves cursor position (not attributes)
- May conflict with some terminal modes

### Layer 3: Emulated (Last Resort)
\`\`\`rust
// Track cursor position explicitly
struct CursorTracker {
    x: u16,
    y: u16,
}

impl CursorTracker {
    fn save(&mut self, current_x: u16, current_y: u16) {
        self.x = current_x;
        self.y = current_y;
    }
    
    fn restore(&self, writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
        write!(writer, "\x1b[{};{}H", self.y + 1, self.x + 1)
    }
}
\`\`\`
- Works everywhere that supports CUP
- Requires tracking cursor position throughout
- More overhead but guaranteed to work

## Strategy Selection
\`\`\`rust
pub enum CursorSaveStrategy {
    Dec,       // ESC 7 / ESC 8
    Ansi,      // CSI s / CSI u
    Emulated,  // Track and CUP
    Auto,      // Detect best for environment
}

impl CursorSaveStrategy {
    pub fn detect(caps: &TerminalCapabilities) -> Self {
        // Most terminals support DEC
        if !caps.in_screen {
            return Self::Dec;
        }
        // Screen has quirks, try ANSI
        Self::Ansi
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Implementation
\`\`\`rust
pub struct CursorManager {
    strategy: CursorSaveStrategy,
    saved_position: Option<(u16, u16)>,  // For emulated mode
}

impl CursorManager {
    pub fn save(&mut self, writer: &mut impl Write, current_pos: (u16, u16)) -> io::Result<()> {
        match self.strategy {
            CursorSaveStrategy::Dec => writer.write_all(DEC_SAVE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Ansi => writer.write_all(ANSI_SAVE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Emulated => {
                self.saved_position = Some(current_pos);
                Ok(())
            }
            CursorSaveStrategy::Auto => {
                // Try DEC, it's most common
                writer.write_all(DEC_SAVE)
            }
        }
    }
    
    pub fn restore(&mut self, writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
        match self.strategy {
            CursorSaveStrategy::Dec => writer.write_all(DEC_RESTORE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Ansi => writer.write_all(ANSI_RESTORE),
            CursorSaveStrategy::Emulated => {
                if let Some((x, y)) = self.saved_position {
                    write!(writer, "\x1b[{};{}H", y + 1, x + 1)?;
                }
                Ok(())
            }
            CursorSaveStrategy::Auto => writer.write_all(DEC_RESTORE),
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## PTY Test: Cursor Restoration
Test that verifies cursor is correctly restored:
1. Move cursor to known position
2. Save cursor
3. Move cursor elsewhere
4. Restore cursor
5. Query position (DSR) and verify matches saved position

## Tests Required
- [ ] DEC save/restore works on xterm-like terminals
- [ ] ANSI save/restore works as fallback
- [ ] Emulated mode tracks and restores correctly
- [ ] Auto-detection chooses appropriate strategy
- [ ] PTY test verifies cursor contract per strategy

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CursorManager implemented with all three strategies
- [ ] Auto-detection based on TerminalCapabilities
- [ ] Integrated into InlinePresenter
- [ ] PTY tests pass for all strategies
- [ ] Documented which terminals use which strategy

## Location

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.14","title":"Demo: Hyperlink Playground (OSC-8 + Hit Regions)","description":"Background
- OSC-8 hyperlinks and hit regions are powerful but under-explained.

Goals
- Display clickable links with hover/focus states.
- Show link registry IDs and OSC-8 sequences for education.
- Demonstrate keyboard navigation among links.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Interaction.
- Short label: "Links".
- Registry tags: "osc8", "hit", "link".

Implementation Notes
- Use LinkRegistry and HitGrid integration.
- Provide deterministic link list with sample URLs.
- Add a "raw sequence" panel for educational display.

Acceptance Criteria
- Links render with distinct styling and focus ring.
- Hover and keyboard focus highlight are visible.
- Optional panel shows link registry ID mapping and sequences.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for link registry behavior in demo state.
- Snapshot tests for normal, hover, and focus states.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with scripted focus movement and JSONL logs:
  - run_id, link_id, focus_idx, action, outcome","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:31.797391729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T02:05:16.757068820Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T02:05:16.757048522Z","close_reason":"Hyperlink Playground screen implemented with OSC-8, hover/focus, link registry panel, JSONL logging + snapshots; included in screen sweep","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.14","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:31.797391729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.15","title":"Demo: Performance Challenge Mode (Degradation Tiers)","description":"Background
- Degradation tiers and budgets are critical but not demonstrated interactively.

Goals
- Provide a "stress" mode that intentionally overwhelms rendering.
- Show real-time degradation tier transitions and recovery.
- Display budget metrics and decision evidence.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Systems.
- Short label: "Perf".
- Registry tags: "budget", "tier", "latency".

Implementation Notes
- Reuse performance HUD metrics and render budget info.
- Add deterministic stress scenarios (fixed seed, fixed workload).
- Include a "cool down" button to show recovery.

Acceptance Criteria
- Stress toggle reliably triggers degradation and recovery.
- UI clearly shows current tier and decision rationale.
- Runs without stalling input handling.
- Evidence entries are recorded for each tier change.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for stress scenario generator determinism.
- Snapshot tests for each degradation tier.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, tier_from, tier_to, frame_time_ms, decision, outcome","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:39.834624358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T03:03:59.234397776Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T03:03:59.234379662Z","close_reason":"Completed performance challenge mode (stress harness + tier logging)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:52:13.446170028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.15","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:39.834624358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.16","title":"Demo: Navigation IA (Screen Registry + Palette + Category Tabs)","description":"Background
- With 30+ screens, navigation can become overwhelming without a strong IA.
- A dedicated registry and palette make the demo feel intentional and fast.

Goals
- Implement Screen Registry (id/title/short_label/category/order/palette_tags/blurb/hotkey).
- Implement category tabs + category-aware navigation.
- Implement Screen Palette (Ctrl+K) with search, filters, favorites, and ranking.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Tour/Core/Visuals/Interaction/Text/Systems displayed with color pills.
- Tab bar shows short_label + category color.
- Shift+Left/Right jumps categories; Left/Right moves within category.

Implementation Notes
- Registry is the single source of truth for screen list and ordering.
- Palette ranking: exact > prefix > word-start > substring (stable tie-breakers).
- Favorites are persisted in memory for session; optional future persistence.

Acceptance Criteria
- Registry drives tab bar, palette, and help overlay (no duplicated lists).
- Palette search is fast and deterministic (fixed ordering for equal scores).
- Category navigation works from any screen.
- All screens are registered with category, label, tags, and blurb.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for registry ordering and palette ranking.
- Snapshot tests for palette (empty, filtered, favorites) at 80x24 and 120x40.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, action, query, selected_screen, category, outcome","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacOwl","created_at":"2026-02-04T03:04:32.705019824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T07:39:45.103372323Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T07:39:45.103354269Z","close_reason":"Completed: palette logging, snapshots, ranking test, help legend, E2E step","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.16","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T03:04:32.705019824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-iuvb.17","title":"Demo UX: Mouse-first interactivity + shortcut expansion","description":"Background
- Demo showcase already has a strong screen catalog, Screen Registry, command palette, and a Guided Tour.
- Mouse support exists but is currently shallow: tab bar click-switching is wired via the hit grid, and a handful of screens register hit regions, but most screens do not respond to mouse in an intuitive 'click what you see' way.
- Many screens have keyboard shortcuts, but lots of interaction is either hidden or passive; the demo should feel alive and exploratory.

Goals
- Make every screen in `ftui-demo-showcase` meaningfully mouse-friendly:
  - Clickable controls/widgets respond as users expect.
  - Scroll wheel scrolls the thing under the pointer (lists, panels, editors).
  - Drag interactions work where appropriate (kanban, drag-drop, timeline scrubbing, sliders).
  - Hover affordances are visible (focus/active state, subtle highlight, status hints).
- Add more keyboard shortcuts that *do things* (not just navigation):
  - Consistent global shortcuts (help, palette, perf HUD, inspector, toggle mouse).
  - Per-screen shortcuts for toggling modes, adjusting knobs, and triggering demos.
- Upgrade the Guided Tour storyboard to include the 'best stuff' and explicitly demonstrate:
  - Inline mode value (scrollback preserved; chrome stable).
  - Determinism + checksums + evidence ledgers.
  - Hit testing + hyperlinks + inspector overlays.
  - Performance HUD + budgets + degradation behavior.

Hard Constraints / Invariants (non-negotiable)
- Preserve Inline Mode scrollback as a first-class property:
  - Default behavior must not silently steal terminal scrollback unless user opts in.
  - Mouse capture policy must be explicit and documented in the demo UI and CLI.
- Keep one-writer rule / terminal lifecycle correctness intact.
- Determinism: fixed seeds + time-freeze remain deterministic across mouse/keyboard interactions.
- Performance: hit testing and hover updates must not materially regress frame time; coalesce high-frequency mouse move events.

Scope Notes
- This epic is about demo UX and interaction plumbing. It may require small changes in `ftui-core`/`ftui-widgets` (e.g., semantic mouse events or richer hit regions) when needed, but avoid large API churn.
- Prefer building shared primitives (clickable list/table/scrollbar/etc.) once, then applying to screens.

Success Criteria
- Every screen under `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/` has:
  - At least one meaningful mouse interaction (click/scroll/drag/hover) that changes state.
  - At least 3-5 screen-specific shortcuts that are discoverable in the help overlay.
- Guided Tour v2 covers the highest-signal screens and interactions.
- Test coverage exists:
  - Unit tests for mouse dispatch + focus/hover rules.
  - Deterministic integration tests per interaction category.
  - PTY/E2E validation where feasible.

Key References (starting points)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (hit grid enablement + tab click handler)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/chrome.rs` (HitId ranges for chrome/panes)
- `crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs` (mouse capture enable/disable)
- `crates/ftui-core/src/semantic_event.rs` (semantic click/drag abstractions)
- `crates/ftui-render/src/frame.rs` + `spatial_hit_index.rs` (hit grid implementation)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"PinkRidge","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:43:01.674582434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:01:08.173325087Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","guided-tour","mouse","shortcuts","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:43:01.674582434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":817,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution Notes / Dependency Shape
- Foundation tasks:
  - bd-iuvb.17.1 (mouse policy / inline scrollback) blocks bd-iuvb.17.2 (dispatcher) and bd-iuvb.17.6 (tour v2) and bd-iuvb.17.7 (tests).
  - bd-iuvb.17.2 (dispatcher) blocks bd-iuvb.17.4 (chrome mouse).
  - bd-iuvb.17.3 (widget hit semantics) and bd-iuvb.17.5 (shortcut standards) are shared prerequisites for essentially all screen work.
- Screen tasks are grouped under sub-epics by ScreenCategory:
  - Tour: bd-iuvb.17.8
  - Core: bd-iuvb.17.9
  - Visuals: bd-iuvb.17.10
  - Interaction: bd-iuvb.17.11
  - Text: bd-iuvb.17.12
  - Systems: bd-iuvb.17.13
- Guided Tour v2 (bd-iuvb.17.6) is explicitly blocked on the highest-signal interactive screens (dashboard, inline mode, determinism, time travel, hyperlinks, perf HUD, etc.) so we don’t ship a tour that skips the good stuff.

Suggested Parallelization
- Do bd-iuvb.17.1 and bd-iuvb.17.2 early, because they define the click/scroll/drag contract.
- bd-iuvb.17.3 (widgets) can proceed in parallel and will unblock many screens quickly.
- Once the dispatcher exists, screens can be upgraded independently by category.","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:58Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","title":"Demo Input: mouse capture policy + inline scrollback preservation","description":"Background
- Mouse capture is currently available (see `ftui-core` terminal session enable/disable and the demo's `--no-mouse` CLI flag), but we must not compromise FrankenTUI's core value proposition: Inline Mode preserves native terminal scrollback.
- In many terminals, enabling mouse reporting steals the scroll wheel from the terminal's scrollback. That is acceptable in AltScreen mode, but it is a footgun in Inline mode unless it is explicit and opt-in.

Goal
- Define and implement a clear, explicit mouse capture policy for the demo showcase that:
  - Preserves native scrollback in Inline mode by default.
  - Still allows a 'full mouse' experience in AltScreen mode by default.
  - Provides an obvious, discoverable toggle and CLI/env config.

Design / Policy (proposed)
- Introduce a demo-level `MouseMode` concept (names are bikesheddable):
  - `Off`: no mouse capture; scroll wheel is terminal scrollback.
  - `On`: mouse capture enabled (click/scroll/drag/hover routed to UI).
- Defaults:
  - AltScreen: `On`.
  - Inline: `Off` (unless user explicitly opts in).
- UI affordances:
  - Status bar shows `Mouse: OFF (scrollback)` vs `Mouse: ON (UI scroll)`.
  - Help overlay documents toggle.
- Controls:
  - Keybinding: `M` toggles mouse mode.
  - CLI: keep `--no-mouse`; add `--mouse=on|off` (or env) if needed for determinism in tests.

Key Constraints
- No surprise scrollback theft in Inline mode.
- Must not violate one-writer rule or terminal teardown invariants.
- Deterministic: mode toggles are explicit inputs; no terminal-dependent heuristics.

Acceptance Criteria
- Inline mode demo retains native scrollback by default (wheel scrolls scrollback).
- AltScreen demo remains fully mouse interactive by default.
- Mouse toggle is visible/discoverable (status + help), and is logged in JSONL if demo logging is enabled.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for CLI/env parsing and default selection based on screen mode.
- PTY E2E smoke: Inline mode launch asserts mouse capture is disabled by default; AltScreen asserts enabled.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs` (Enable/Disable mouse capture)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/cli.rs` (mouse flags)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (event routing)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.578455672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:20:08.430042660Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:16:01.310846497Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","inline-mode","input","mouse","scrollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.578455672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":815,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CloudyGorge claiming this bead. Will implement mouse capture policy with MouseMode enum, screen-mode-aware defaults, M keybinding toggle, status bar indicator, and CLI flags.","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:39Z"},{"id":833,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","author":"EmeraldAnchor","text":"Note: ftui-runtime still has a stale TODO/no-op for mouse capture. In crates/ftui-runtime/src/program.rs:2191-2195, Cmd::SetMouseCapture(enabled) currently ignores enabled; TerminalSession already has set_mouse_capture(bool) in crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs:393. Wiring execute_cmd to call self.session.set_mouse_capture(enabled)? should unblock demo mouse-mode work.","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:14:38Z"},{"id":837,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FoggyFalcon: Completed demo-showcase integration for mouse capture policy. Changes: CLI --mouse=on|off|auto flag, resolve_mouse_enabled() in main.rs, ToggleMouseCapture msg/handler with Cmd::SetMouseCapture, F6/m keybindings, status bar Mouse:ON/OFF indicator, help overlay entry, command palette action, Down+Up mouse click infrastructure, all 1427 lib tests + 244 snapshot tests passing.","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:20:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","title":"Mouse/Shortcuts Pass: Visuals screens","description":"Purpose
- Track mouse-first + shortcut-rich upgrades for Visuals-category screens.

Included Screens
- Data Viz
- Table Theme Gallery
- Mermaid Showcase
- Mermaid Mega Showcase
- Visual Effects
- Theme Studio
- Snapshot Player (Time Travel Studio)

Visuals Interaction Themes
- Mouse for pan/zoom/inspect where meaningful (diagrams, charts).
- Wheel adjusts scale or scrolls within the appropriate panel, never 'randomly'.
- Keyboard shortcuts trigger parameter sweeps, overlays, and reset-to-deterministic-state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:54.931165079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:54.931165079Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts","visuals"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:54.931165079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","title":"Data Viz: click-to-toggle series + mouse inspect + shortcuts","description":"Background
- DataViz is a dense visuals screen; it should support mouse inspection and fast toggles.

Goal
- Make charts feel interactive: click legends, hover points, wheel scroll/zoom where appropriate.

Mouse Interactions
- Hover a chart point shows tooltip (in status hint or overlay).
- Click legend toggles series visibility.
- Wheel adjusts zoom scale or scrolls within the selected chart panel (consistent rule).

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `L`: toggle legend.
- `Z`: toggle zoom mode.
- `R`: reset view.

Acceptance Criteria
- Interactions are deterministic (same inputs -> same tooltip/selection).
- At least 3-5 shortcuts are available and discoverable.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/data_viz.rs`","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["data-viz","demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
